channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: School of Architecture Co-terminal Information Session
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 10, 2026 1:00 PM - 2:00 PM - Are you interested in opportunities to pursue graduate studies? Join us for a slice of pizza and a chance to hear about the different master’s degrees that the School of Architecture offers at one of the upcoming information sessions. We’ll talk about the benefits of co-terminal programs and the professional benefits of graduating with a master’s degree. Interested students are encouraged to attend. The co-terminal application deadline is March…
item.author: Students

item.title: UPWARDS US-Japan Virtual STEM Networking Event - February 9 at 7:00pm
item.description: Date: Monday, February 2, 2026 12:00 AM - Mon , February 9, 2026 6:30 PM - Join us for the next UPWARDS US-Japan STEM Networking event. The event will begin with mini-lectures by guest speakers on their graduate school experience and career, followed by time to network with students from UPWARDS partner universities. Speakers: Dr. Lan (Samantha) Li, Professor, Boise State University and Dr. Esther Wertz, Associate Professor, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute Register: https://zoom.us/meeting…
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI at Work Hosts Graduate Certificate Review on March 10
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 10, 2026 7:00 PM - 7:30 PM - Join RPI at Work Dean Aric Krause, Ph.D., in a 30-minute information session highlighting the whole range of RPI’s online, project-based graduate certificate programs. These certificates are three courses in length, developing targeted ability groups, and can be completed in as little as seven months. Please complete the form below if you plan to attend.
item.author: Community

item.title: Concert on March 11 - CODE POEMS: Migrations and Angels
item.description: The electroacoustic duo CODE POEMS, featuring Matt Sargent (composition, guitar, electronics) and Taylor Long (percussion), is dedicated to performing experimental works by living composers. The program features two pieces written expressly for the ensemble: Matthew Goodheart’s “Migrations,” for percussion and lap steel with multichannel electronics, and Matt Sargent’s “Several Angels,” for percussion and electric guitar with real-time scoring. Join at RPI Chapel and Cultural Center from 7-8:30.
item.author: Community

item.title: DotCIO requests feedback on the student laptop program and for help selecting next year’s student laptops
item.description: The Mobile Computing Program is asking for feedback from the community to help select the most appropriate laptop models and specifications for this coming year. Additionally, we are seeking general feedback on the program. Please take a few minutes to complete this survey which is open through March 7, 2026.
item.author: Community

item.title: Arthur G. Schultz Memorial Symposium
item.description: Date: Monday, March 9, 2026 8:00 AM - 9:00 AM - Arthur Schultz’s legacy will be honored with talks from several former group members weaving chemistry and careers together, highlighting the impact he had on their career path. The program begins at 8:00 am and concludes at 5:30 pm. Please contact Sharon Gardner (derris@rpi.edu) for additional information.
item.author: Community

item.title: DotCIO - Server Maintenance Scheduled starting 9am on 03/07/2026
item.description: Due to necessary software maintenance, a scheduled outage will take place beginning at 9:00AM on Saturday, March 7th with an expected duration of 3-5 hours. During this time, some computing services may be unavailable. It should be noted that this will NOT be a network outage. Please click in for additional details and link to report if issues are experienced after this time frame.
item.author: Community

item.title: Exciting News: Ice Rink Project at Houston Field House Begins March 6
item.description: Dear Campus Community, We are pleased to announce that the long-anticipated ice rink renovation project at the Houston Field House at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute will officially commence on Friday, March 6, with substantial completion anticipated by early September. This significant investment represents an exciting new chapter for our campus and for RPI’s proud and competitive hockey tradition. The upgraded rink and supporting systems will enhance performance, safety, and the overall…
item.author: Community

item.title: Ice and Refrigeration System Renovation HFH – Site Logistics Plan
item.description: Dear Campus Community, We are pleased to announce that the long-anticipated ice rink floor and refrigeration system renovation at the Houston Field House at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute will officially commence on Friday, March 6, with substantial completion anticipated by early September. This significant investment represents an exciting new chapter for our campus and for RPI’s proud and competitive hockey tradition. The upgraded rink and supporting systems will enhance performance,…
item.author: Community

item.title: 03/11/2026 Onboarding session for Alorithmiq’s Tensor-Network Error Mitigation used with Quantum
item.description: RPI has licensed Alorithmiq’s Tensor-Network Error Mitigation (TEM) Qiskit function for use with our dedicated IBM Quantum System One and Premium IBM Quantum Cloud account. An onboarding session is scheduled for Wednesday March 11th, 9:30 AM in CII 3206. Please click in for additional details.
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday March 13: Webex Event Training
item.description: Hosting a campus event or supporting a student webinar? Join a hands-on Webex Event Training Webinar, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. Register now for this webinar presented via Webex on Friday March 13 from 11am-12pm: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700 Learn the basics of Webex Event hosting, with demonstration and Q&A, along with new features recently added to the hosting platform.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Seminar: Supervisory Essentials
item.description:
In this seminar, Managers and Supervisors will learn from each other and our Facilitators about important topics such as the Employee Value Proposition and Employee Experience, the six fundamental Leadership competencies at Rensselaer, and experience a “day in the life” of a Supervisor. In addition, the importance of managing employee performance, providing both rewards and recognition, employment law fundamentals and tools for building team capacity will be explored. For more information…

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
